message:
test(fcp-core): trybuild compile-fail tests for typestate split (br-jkcka.6)

Adds a trybuild test suite that proves the type-level guarantee from
jkcka.3: an `UnboundVerified` token CANNOT flow to a function that
requires `BoundVerified`. This is enforced at compile time.

Adds:
- `trybuild = "1"` dev-dep in fcp-core
- `tests/typestate_compile_fail.rs` — trybuild runner
- `tests/ui/unbound_cannot_reach_bound_api.rs` + .stderr — the negative
  case. Compiler error is exactly the mismatched-types diagnostic,
  naming both markers.
- `tests/ui/promote_path_compiles.rs` — the positive case: gateway
  produces unbound, connector promotes, executor accepts bound. The
  CORRECT code must compile.

Together these tests lock in the jkcka invariant so a future
refactor cannot silently widen the type surface (e.g., making
BoundVerified publicly constructible without verifier passage, or
adding a generic conversion impl that bypasses promote_with_instance).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,6 +39,7 @@ criterion.workspace = true
 jsonschema.workspace = true
 semver.workspace = true
 rand = "0.9"
+trybuild = "1"
 
 [[bench]]
 name = "pcs_benchmarks"

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+//! jkcka.6 — trybuild compile-fail tests for the capability-token typestate.
+//!
+//! These tests assert that **incorrect code does not compile**, which is
+//! the whole point of typestate. A future refactor that silently widens
+//! the type surface (e.g., making `BoundVerified` publicly constructible
+//! without a verifier) will fail these tests.
+//!
+//! Each `.rs` fixture in `tests/ui/` is a self-contained compile attempt.
+//! `trybuild` runs them and diffs stderr against checked-in `.stderr`
+//! expectations.
+
+#[test]
+fn typestate_enforces_bound_vs_unbound_at_compile_time() {
+    let t = trybuild::TestCases::new();
+    t.compile_fail("tests/ui/unbound_cannot_reach_bound_api.rs");
+    t.pass("tests/ui/promote_path_compiles.rs");
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+// Compile-fail fixture: passing CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified> to a
+// function that requires CapabilityToken<BoundVerified> must fail to
+// compile.
+//
+// This is the core type-level guarantee from the jkcka epic — a token
+// that has only been gateway-verified (4/5 checks) cannot reach a
+// function that expects full (5/5) enforcement.
+
+use fcp_core::{BoundVerified, CapabilityToken, UnboundVerified};
+
+fn takes_bound(_: CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>) {}
+
+fn main() {
+    // Fabricate an UnboundVerified token reference. We can't actually
+    // construct one outside the verifier, but we can reference the
+    // type in an uninitialized binding for the purposes of the
+    // compile error the test wants to assert.
+    let t: CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified> = unimplemented!();
+    takes_bound(t);
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+warning: unreachable statement
+  --> tests/ui/unbound_cannot_reach_bound_api.rs:19:5
+   |
+18 |     let t: CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified> = unimplemented!();
+   |                                               ---------------- any code following this expression is unreachable
+19 |     takes_bound(t);
+   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ unreachable statement
+   |
+   = note: `#[warn(unreachable_code)]` (part of `#[warn(unused)]`) on by default
+
+error[E0308]: mismatched types
+  --> tests/ui/unbound_cannot_reach_bound_api.rs:19:17
+   |
+19 |     takes_bound(t);
+   |     ----------- ^ expected `CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>`, found `CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>`
+   |     |
+   |     arguments to this function are incorrect
+   |
+   = note: expected struct `CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>`
+              found struct `CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>`
+note: function defined here
+  --> tests/ui/unbound_cannot_reach_bound_api.rs:11:4
+   |
+11 | fn takes_bound(_: CapabilityToken<BoundVerified>) {}
+   |    ^^^^^^^^^^^ ---------------------------------
